message:
perf(planner): swap Arc<QueryPlan> for Rc<QueryPlan> in the plan cache

\`QueryPlanner\` is a per-connection object — the plan cache
lives inside a single connection and is never handed across
thread boundaries. The \`plan_cache: LruCache<u64,
Arc<QueryPlan>>\` declaration therefore paid atomic
ref-counting on every hit for no benefit: every \`Arc::clone\`
was an \`fetch_add\` + \`fetch_sub\` pair that Rc can do with
plain register operations.

Swap the type everywhere plans are shared WITHIN the planner:

- \`plan_cache\` stores \`Rc<QueryPlan>\`.
- \`cached_plan\` builds plans via \`Rc::new(build())\` and
  returns \`Rc::clone(plan)\` on hit.
- \`order_joins_with_planner\` builds via \`Rc::new(...)\` and
  returns \`Rc::clone(plan)\` on hit — the three places
  \`Arc\` appeared in the body are now \`Rc\`.
- Public return types follow: \`cached_plan\` and
  \`order_joins_with_planner\` now return \`Rc<QueryPlan>\`. The
  only external callers are inside this crate and the
  benchmark below, both updated together.

Imports cleaned up:

- \`std::rc::Rc\` imported.
- \`std::sync::Arc\` dropped from the imports list in
  \`planner/src/lib.rs\` — nothing else in the file uses Arc.

Benchmark \`benches/plan_cache_hot_paths.rs\`:

- Follows the return-type change. \`Arc::clone\` →
  \`Rc::clone\` and \`Arc::ptr_eq\` → \`Rc::ptr_eq\` at the two
  \`bench_cached_plan_hit\` / \`bench_order_joins_with_cache_hit\`
  sites.
- Imports: \`std::sync::Arc\` out, \`std::rc::Rc\` in.

No public API semantic change beyond the return-type rename —
no call site that previously relied on \`Send + Sync\` on a
\`QueryPlan\` handle exists today. Cache hit cost drops by the
two atomic ops that every \`Arc::clone\` was performing.
